## Major Fix #8 — Add Recurring Characters, NOTE Admonitions & DA Guidance

**Status:** Should fix before publication  
**Affects:** All mechanical chapters (03, 05, 06, 07, 08, 10, 13, 15, 18, 19)

### Problem
The rulebook lacks the two things that make Neon Relic feel professional and usable:

1. **No recurring named characters in examples.** Neon Relic uses Petra Vasquez, Yusuf Demir, and Agent Reyes across multiple chapters. Readers build familiarity. HoL has two one-off examples (Kael attacks a goblin in ch06, Makeva is created in ch02) that never appear again.

2. **Zero NOTE admonitions.** Neon Relic uses 60+ NOTE/TIP/WARNING blocks for rules clarifications, design philosophy, and DA guidance. HoL has none — every rule is flat text.

3. **No DA guidance sections.** Neon Relic chapters include explicit "when to roll," "how to adjudicate," and "common mistakes" sections. HoL chapters have none.

### Implementation

#### Part A: Create a Cast of Example Characters
Design 4 recurring characters who appear in every worked example:

| Character | Class | Ancestry | Personality Hook |
|-----------|-------|----------|-----------------|
| **Kael** | Blade | Human | Hot-headed but loyal. Uses Fire Disciplines. |
| **Lyra** | Intellect | Elf | Curious scholar. Always identifying things. |
| **Roric** | Protector | Dwarf | Stoic defender. The party's shield. |
| **Zara** | Arcanist | — | Quiet and intense. Wind and Energy specialist. |

Update the opening fiction (chapter 01b) to introduce them as a party. Then use them in every worked example across all chapters:
- Chapter 03: Kael demonstrates Brawn, Lyra demonstrates Knowledge
- Chapter 06: Kael's attack, Lyra's skill check, Roric's critical, Zara's fumble
- Chapter 07: Show each character's skill choices
- Chapter 08: Show Kael earning Blade Disciplines, Zara's spell chain progression
- Chapter 10: Zara casting Firebolt → Fireball → Volcanic Eruption
- Chapter 13: Full party combat example
- Chapter 18: The party's advancement after a major quest

#### Part B: Add NOTE Admonitions
Audit every chapter and add at minimum 2-3 NOTE blocks per chapter. Categories:

| Type | Example |
|------|---------|
| **Rules Clarification** | "The Grappled condition does not prevent the target from attacking — only from moving. A grappled spellcaster can still cast." |
| **Design Philosophy** | "Attacks always hit because missing feels bad, and rolling twice slows combat. This system makes every swing matter." |
| **DA Guidance** | "Don't call for a roll when the outcome isn't in doubt. If the rogue has unlimited time and the right tools, they pick the lock. The roll is for when the guards are approaching." |
| **Common Mistake** | "New DAs often apply difficulty modifiers to the tier boundaries instead of the roll. Modifiers shift the 3d6 total — the tier boundaries (1-6, 7-12, 13-18+) are fixed." |
| **Table Wisdom** | "If a player asks 'can I try again?' and there's no consequence for failure, you shouldn't have asked for a roll in the first place." |

#### Part C: Add DA Guidance Sections
Each mechanical chapter needs a "Running This" section at the end:

- Chapter 06: "When to Roll — And When Not To"
- Chapter 07: "Adjudicating Skill Checks"
- Chapter 08: "Managing Discipline Progression"
- Chapter 10: "Pacing Magic in Your Campaign"
- Chapter 13: "Combat Pacing and When to End a Fight"
- Chapter 18: "Advancement Pacing and Milestone Alternatives"

### Success Criteria
- 4 named characters appear in at least 3 examples each
- At least 2 NOTE blocks per mechanical chapter (minimum 20 total)
- DA guidance section at end of every mechanical chapter
- Characters' mechanical choices are consistent across examples (Kael's stats don't change between chapters)
